input.headline: First kiss dates back 21 million years, say scientists

raw.headline: First kiss dates back 21 million years, say scientists

winner: A prehistoric couple, on their first kiss, asked each other, "Do these rocks taste funny to you?

candidates.text: Why did the dinosaur have such a bad date? Because it was 21 million years of small talk before the first kiss!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative use of the headline's time frame, good pun on 'small talk,' and ties dinosaurs to the joke naturally."}

candidates.text: Two dinosaurs were about to have their first kiss when suddenly one said, "I just wanna fossil around!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'fossil' and 'fool around' are clever and specific, aligning well with the headline's scientific twist on a romantic moment."}

candidates.text: A caveman and a cavewoman finally managed to coordinate their schedules for a date... after 21 million meetings.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative twist on the headline with a humorous take on scheduling and evolution"}

candidates.text: Scientists have confirmed that the first kiss was a Neanderthal's prank... It left its victim quite stoned for millions of years!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative with the Neanderthal prank concept, but 'stoned for millions of years' feels forced and less surprising. It lacks precision in connecting to the headline and has a slightly awkward punchline."}

candidates.text: A prehistoric couple, on their first kiss, asked each other, "Do these rocks taste funny to you?
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the pun on 'rocks' feels forced and lacks natural flow. The setup could be more tightly connected to the headline for greater impact."}
